If omitted, the tool will attempt to resolve the target from the current workflow context (triggering issue, PR, or discussion). If omitted, removes labels from the item that triggered this workflow.","type":"number"},"labels":{"description":"Label names to remove (e.g., ['bug', 'needs-triage']). Labels that don't exist on the item are silently skipped. At least one label must be provided.","items":{"type":"string"},"minItems":1,"type":"array"},"secrecy":{"description":"Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., \"public\", \"internal\", \"private\").","type":"string"}},"required":["labels"],"type":"object"},"name":"remove_labels"},{"description":"Assign one or more GitHub users to an issue. Use this to delegate work to specific team members. Users must have access to the repository.","inputSchema":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{"assignee":{"description":"Single GitHub username to assign. Use 'assignees' array for multiple users.","type":"string"},"assignees":{"description":"GitHub usernames to assign to the issue (e.g., ['octocat', 'mona']). Users must have access to the repository.","items":{"type":"string"},"type":"array"},"integrity":{"description":"Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., \"low\", \"medium\", \"high\").","type":"string"},"issue_number":{"description":"Issue number to assign users to. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 543 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/543). If omitted, assigns to the issue that triggered this workflow.","type":["number","string"]},"secrecy":{"description":"Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., \"public\", \"internal\", \"private\").","type":"string"}},"required":["issue_number"],"type":"object"},"name":"assign_to_user"},{"description":"Report that a tool or capability needed to complete the task is not available, or share any information you deem important about missing functionality or limitations. Use this when you cannot accomplish what was requested because the required functionality is missing or access is restricted.","inputSchema":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{"alternatives":{"description":"Any workarounds, manual steps, or alternative approaches the user could take (max 256 characters).","type":"string"},"integrity":{"description":"Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., \"low\", \"medium\", \"high\").","type":"string"},"reason":{"description":"Explanation of why this tool is needed or what information you want to share about the limitation (max 256 characters).","type":"string"},"secrecy":{"description":"Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., \"public\", \"internal\", \"private\").","type":"string"},"tool":{"description":"Optional: Name or description of the missing tool or capability (max 128 characters). Be specific about what functionality is needed.","type":"string"}},"required":["reason"],"type":"object"},"name":"missing_tool"},{"description":"Log a transparency message when no significant actions are needed. Use this to confirm workflow completion and provide visibility when analysis is complete but no changes or outputs are required (e.g., 'No issues found', 'All checks passed'). This ensures the workflow produces human-visible output even when no other actions are taken.","inputSchema":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{"integrity":{"description":"Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., \"low\", \"medium\", \"high\").","type":"string"},"message":{"description":"Status or completion message to log. Should explain what was analyzed and the outcome (e.g., 'Code review complete - no issues found', 'Analysis complete - all tests passing').","type":"string"},"secrecy":{"description":"Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., \"public\", \"internal\", \"private\").","type":"string"}},"required":["message"],"type":"object"},"name":"noop"},{"description":"Report that data or information needed to complete the task is not available. Use this when you cannot accomplish what was requested because required data, context, or information is missing.","inputSchema":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{"alternatives":{"description":"Any workarounds, manual steps, or alternative approaches the user could take (max 256 characters).","type":"string"},"context":{"description":"Additional context about the missing data or where it should come from (max 256 characters).","type":"string"},"data_type":{"description":"Type or description of the missing data or information (max 128 characters). Be specific about what data is needed.","type":"string"},"integrity":{"description":"Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., \"low\", \"medium\", \"high\").","type":"string"},"reason":{"description":"Explanation of why this data is needed to complete the task (max 256 characters).","type":"string"},"secrecy":{"description":"Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., \"public\", \"internal\", \"private\").","type":"string"}},"required":[],"type":"object"},"name":"missing_data"},{"description":"Signal that the task could not be completed due to an infrastructure or tool failure (e.g., MCP server crash, missing authentication, inaccessible repository). Use this when required tools or data are unavailable and the task cannot be meaningfully performed. This is distinct from noop (no action needed) — it indicates an active failure that prevented the task from running. The workflow framework will treat this as a failure signal even when the agent exits successfully.","inputSchema":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{"details":{"description":"Optional extended details or diagnostic context about the failure (max 65000 characters).","type":"string"},"reason":{"description":"A concise explanation of why the task could not be completed (max 1024 characters). Be specific about which tools or resources were unavailable.","type":"string"}},"required":["reason"],"type":"object"},"name":"report_incomplete"},{"description":"Post a routing comment @mentioning team owners on the triggering issue; bypasses safe-outputs mention neutralization","inputSchema":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{"message":{"description":"The comment body text without any @mentions or @ symbols","type":"string"},"owners":{"description":"Comma-separated GitHub usernames to notify, without the @ prefix (e.g. 'user1, user2, Azure/team-name')","type":"string"}},"required":["message","owners"],"type":"object"},"name":"mention_owners"}]}}}

2026-04-08T03:18:10.8448562Z {"timestamp":"2026-04-08T03:16:56.686699976Z","direction":"IN","type":"RESPONSE","server_id":"github","payload":{"id":1,"jsonrpc":"2.0","result":{"content":[{"text":"{\"number\":57913,\"title\":\"[AI Projects Agents] Docs: README Toolboxes section missing client creation and key operations\",\"body\":\"## Documentation Gap\\n\\n**Package:** `Azure.AI.Projects.Agents`\\n**Service directory:** `sdk/ai/Azure.AI.Projects.Agents/`\\n**Triggered by:** PR #57780 by `@nick863`\\n\\n## What Changed\\n\\nPR #57780 added the new `AgentToolboxes` client to `Azure.AI.Projects.Agents`. This includes:\\n- New `AgentToolboxes` client class (both custom and generated) exposing CRUD operations for toolboxes\\n- A new companion sample file `samples/Sample_Toolboxes_CRUD.md` with comprehensive, step-by-step instructions for all operations\\n- A test-backed sample file `tests/Samples/Sample_TooboxesCRUD.cs` with `#region Snippet:` tags for code extraction\\n- An update to `README.md` adding a `### Toolboxes` subsection\\n\\n## Gaps Found\\n\\n- **Missing client initialization step in README Toolboxes section**: The `### Toolboxes` section in the README jumps directly to `toolboxClient.CreateToolboxVersionAsync(...)` without showing how to obtain the `AgentToolboxes` client. Users must know to call `agentsClient.GetAgentToolboxes()` and, critically, that they need to attach the `FeaturePolicy(\\u0026#34;Toolboxes=V1Preview\\u0026#34;)` experimental header to the client options — this is documented only in the standalone `samples/Sample_Toolboxes_CRUD.md` but not in the README.\\n- **Missing operations in README Toolboxes section**: Only Create and Get operations are shown. The following operations are available but not referenced in the README at all: update toolbox default version (`UpdateToolboxAsync`), list all toolbox versions (`GetToolboxVersionsAsync`), list all toolboxes (`GetToolboxesAsync`), and delete toolbox/version (`DeleteToolboxAsync`, `DeleteToolboxVersionAsync`). The standalone `samples/` file covers all of these.\\n- **Wrong package name in Install section**: `### Install the package` shows `dotnet add package Azure.AI.Extensions.OpenAI --prerelease` — this is the wrong package. It should be `dotnet add package Azure.AI.Projects.Agents --prerelease`. There is also an irrelevant Cosmos DB note that belongs to a different package.\\n- **TOC/heading mismatch**: The table of contents lists `[Prompt Agents](#prompt-agents)` but the actual section heading under `## Examples` is `## Declarative Agents`. The wrong heading level (`##` instead of `###`) also breaks the section hierarchy.\\n- **Duplicate snippet in README**: The `Sample_Agents_CreateAgentVersionCRUD_Async` code block appears twice — once in the `Additional concepts` section and again under the `## Declarative Agents` section.\\n- **Test sample file typo**: `tests/Samples/Sample_TooboxesCRUD.cs` has a double-\\u0026#39;o\\u0026#39; typo (should be `Sample_ToolboxesCRUD.cs`).\\n\\n\\n\\u003cstrong\\u003e📐 Implementation Guide\\u003c/strong\\u003e\\n\\nThis section contains step-by-step instructions for a coding agent to implement the changes described above.\\n\\n### Step 1: Modify files\\n\\n#### File: `sdk/ai/Azure.AI.Projects.Agents/README.md`\\n\\n**Edit – Fix Install section (wrong package name and irrelevant Cosmos DB note):**\\n\\nReplace:\\n```markdown\\n```\\ndotnet add package Azure.AI.Extensions.OpenAI --prerelease\\n```\\n\\n\\u0026gt; You must have an [Azure subscription]((azure.microsoft.com/redacted) and [Cosmos DB account]((docs.microsoft.com/redacted) (SQL API). In order to take advantage of the C# 8.0 syntax, it is recommended that you compile using the [.NET Core SDK]((dotnet.microsoft.com/redacted) 3.0 or higher with a [language version]((docs.microsoft.com/redacted) of `latest`.  It is also possible to compile with the .NET Core SDK 2.1.x using a language version of `preview`.\\n```\\n\\nWith:\\n```markdown\\n```\\ndotnet add package Azure.AI.Projects.Agents --prerelease\\n```\\n```\\n\\n**Edit – Fix TOC entry `Prompt Agents` → `Declarative Agents`:**\\n\\nIn the Table of contents, replace:\\n```markdown\\n  - [Prompt Agents](#prompt-agents)\\n```\\nWith:\\n```markdown\\n  - [Declarative Agents](#declarative-agents)\\n```\\n\\n**Edit – Fix Examples heading level for Declarative Agents:**\\n\\nReplace:\\n```markdown\\n## Declarative Agents\\n```\\nWith:\\n```markdown\\n### Declarative Agents\\n```\\n\\n**Edit – Remove the duplicate `Sample_Agents_CreateAgentVersionCRUD_Async` snippet** that appears for the second time in the `## Declarative Agents` section (keep only the one in `Additional concepts`).\\n\\n**Edit – Expand the `### Toolboxes` section** to include client initialization and the missing operations. Replace the existing `### Toolboxes` section with:\\n\\n```markdown\\n### Toolboxes\\n\\nToolboxes allow us to store tools in Azure so that they can be retrieved and used by the Agents.\\nAs for the Hosted Agent we will need to set the experimental header, but in this scenario the header is `Toolboxes=V1Preview`. We also need to disable the `AAIP001` warning.\\n\\nFirst, create the `AgentToolboxes` client by adding the experimental header policy and calling `GetAgentToolboxes()`:\\n\\n```\\n```\\n\\nIn the example below we create two versions of an MCP tool and save them to Azure:\\n\\n```\\n```\\n\\nThere are two objects which help to work with Toolboxes: `ToolboxRecord` and `ToolboxVersion`. `ToolboxRecord` can be retrieved by name; it contains the default version of the Toolbox:\\n\\n```\\n```\\n\\nThe name of the Toolbox and its version allow you to get the `ToolboxVersion` object, which contains the tools that can be used by an Agent:\\n\\n```\\n```\\n\\nTo update the default version of a `ToolboxRecord`:\\n\\n```\\n```\\n\\nList all versions of a toolbox:\\n\\n```\\n```\\n\\nList all toolboxes:\\n\\n```\\n```\\n\\nFinally, delete toolbox versions and toolboxes:\\n\\n```\\n```\\n```\\n\\n### Step 2: Add or update code snippets\\n\\nThe snippet tags already exist in the test file `sdk/ai/Azure.AI.Projects.Agents/tests/Samples/Sample_TooboxesCRUD.cs`. The README placeholder blocks added in Step 1 (e.g., `` ```C# Snippet:Sample_UpdateToolbox_ToolboxesAgentsCRUD_Async ``) will be filled in automatically by `Update-Snippets.ps1`. No manual code pasting is needed.\\n\\n\\u0026gt; **Note:** If renaming the test file to fix the typo (`Sample_TooboxesCRUD.cs` → `Sample_ToolboxesCRUD.cs`), update any project references accordingly.\\n\\n### Step 3: Verify README structure\\n\\nThe README has all required sections, but verify them after edits:\\n1. ✅ Getting started (Install the package, Prerequisites, Authenticate the client) — **fix wrong package name**\\n2. ✅ Key concepts\\n3. ✅ Examples — **fix heading level and TOC entry; expand Toolboxes subsection**\\n4. ✅ Troubleshooting\\n5. ✅ Next steps\\n6. ✅ Contributing\\n\\nUse `sdk/keyvault/Azure.Security.KeyVault.Secrets/README.md` as the canonical reference.\\n\\n### Step 4: Validate\\n\\nRun these commands in order:\\n\\n1. `dotnet build sdk/ai/Azure.AI.Projects.Agents/`\\n2. `dotnet test sdk/ai/Azure.AI.Projects.Agents/ --filter TestCategory!=Live`\\n3. `eng/scripts/Update-Snippets.ps1 ai` — confirms README snippet placeholders are filled from test file regions\\n4. `eng/scripts/Export-API.ps1 ai` — only needed if public API surface changed (no changes expected for this doc fix)\\n5. `dotnet format sdk/ai/Azure.AI.Projects.Agents/`\\n\\n\\n\\n## Next Steps\\n\\n\\u0026gt;
